response:
Nurse Emily organized a creative writing workshop for the 24 residents in her care facility. She divided them into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To ensure a productive session, she planned to allocate 3 writing prompts per group, which means 3 × 4 = 12 prompts for the initial set. 

To accommodate the possibility of the groups wanting more, Emily prepared an additional set of 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. This means each group can receive 10 ÷ 4 = 2.5 prompts, but since prompts cannot be split, the extra set is available for the groups to use as needed. 

In total, Emily needs to have 12 initial prompts plus 10 extra prompts, resulting in 22 writing prompts. The library at the facility will be a valuable resource for the residents to access additional writing materials if needed. The library also serves as a quiet space where participants can reflect on their writing. 

The END.
